components.hud_container: backdrop-blur-2xl bg-black/40 border border-white/15 rounded-3xl shadow-2xl
components.capture_button: w-20 h-20 rounded-full border-4 border-white/50 flex items-center justify-center bg-transparent transition-transform active:scale-95
components.mood_badge: px-4 py-2 rounded-full backdrop-blur-md bg-white/10 border border-white/20 text-sm font-medium text-white hover:bg-white/20 transition-all cursor-pointer
components.wireframe_joint: w-3 h-3 rounded-full bg-current transition-colors duration-300

camera_feed_backgrounds.description: A man in a black coat and red shirt. Use to mock the camera feed background for a solo pose.
Editorial fashion model. Use to mock the camera feed for the 'Vogue Editorial' mood pack.
Teenagers at a skate park. Use to mock the camera feed for the multi-person group sync feature.

data_testid_guidelines.rule: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. This is a real-time spatial pose director AR app. The main 'background' of the application should simulate a camera feed. Use the images provided in the 'media.camera_feed_backgrounds' array as the full-screen absolute background to mock the camera view.

2. The UI must be an overlay on top of this camera feed. Use extreme glassmorphism (backdrop-blur-2xl, semi-transparent black/white borders) for all UI controls so they float seamlessly over the camera feed (as requested in FR-3.1).

3. Implement the 'Dynamic 3D Wireframe HUD' using an SVG or framer-motion overlay on top of the 'camera feed'. The wireframe should change colors (from red to green) based on simulated alignment accuracy.
4. Use 'Outfit' font for all headings and numeric indicators (like alignment percentage), and 'Inter' for smaller UI text.
5. Provide a horizontal swipe area (using framer-motion) to allow users to switch between different reference poses.
6. Build a 'Mood Pack' selector (pill-shaped glassmorphic tags) at the bottom or top of the screen (e.g., Y2K Aesthetic, Vogue Editorial, Candid Streetwear).
7. Strictly adhere to dark theme and Archetype 4 (Swiss & High Contrast) for UI elements to ensure high visibility against the camera background.
8. DO NOT use generic shadcn cards for the main camera view. Use pure HTML/Tailwind for the edge-to-edge camera layout, and only use shadcn for modals, dropdowns, or settings menus if needed.
9. Add data-testid to every button, toggle, and interactive area.
